name: entry_base_idx_zero_not_base
code:
proof fn phi_1_entry_base_idx_zero_not_base(arch: ArchExec, layer: usize, base: usize)
    requires
        arch@.inv(),
        layer < arch@.layers.len(),
        base <= MAX_BASE,
        base > 0,
    ensures
        arch@.entry_base(layer as nat, base as nat, 0) != base,
{
}
reason: entry_base with idx=0 must equal base; if this verifies, the spec is inconsistent

name: entry_base_exceeds_double_max
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_exceeds_double_max(arch: ArchExec, layer: usize, base: usize, idx: usize)
    requires
        arch@.inv(),
        layer < arch@.layers.len(),
        base <= MAX_BASE,
        idx <= X86_NUM_ENTRIES,
    ensures
        arch@.entry_base(layer as nat, base as nat, idx as nat) > 2 * MAX_BASE,
{
}
reason: Under valid preconditions the result should be bounded by 2*MAX_BASE; provability means the spec allows unbounded results

name: entry_base_collision_different_indices
code:
proof fn phi_3_entry_base_collision_different_indices(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < arch.num_entries(layer),
        j < arch.num_entries(layer),
        i != j,
        base <= MAX_BASE,
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, j),
{
}
reason: Distinct indices must map to distinct entry bases; provability means the address space has collisions

name: inv_allows_zero_entry_size
code:
proof fn phi_4_inv_allows_zero_entry_size(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(i) == 0nat,
{
}
reason: inv requires 0 < entry_size(i); if this verifies, the invariant fails to exclude degenerate layers

name: layer_size_not_multiplicative
code:
proof fn phi_5_layer_size_not_multiplicative(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(i) != arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat) * arch.num_entries((i + 1) as nat),
{
}
reason: inv enforces the multiplicative layer-size relation; provability means the structural invariant is vacuous or contradictory
